questions.question: Why did Mahendra prefer to keep Iswaran with him?
questions.answer: Because Iswaran cooked for him and kept him entertained with stories.

questions.question: How did Iswaran manage to cook delicious meals even in remote places?
questions.answer: He somehow arranged vegetables and ingredients even in deserted places.

questions.question: How did Iswaran describe a fallen tree on the road?
questions.answer: He described it dramatically as a huge bushy beast lying across the road.

questions.question: Why did Mahendra enjoy listening to Iswaran’s stories?
questions.answer: Because they were narrated with suspense and excitement.

questions.question: What effect did Tamil thriller books have on Iswaran?
questions.answer: They influenced his dramatic storytelling style.

questions.question: Why did the elephant enter the town in Iswaran’s story?
questions.answer: Because it had escaped from the timber yard and gone mad.

questions.question: How did people react to the mad elephant?
questions.answer: They ran away in panic and hid.

questions.question: What did the elephant do in the school ground?
questions.answer: It destroyed the goal post, volleyball net and other objects.

questions.question: Why did Mahendra have no television?
questions.answer: Because he lived in temporary shelters at remote worksites.

questions.question: Why did Iswaran prepare special food one night?
questions.answer: Because it was an auspicious day for feeding ancestral spirits.

questions.question: Why did Mahendra become uneasy after hearing the ghost story?
questions.answer: Because the story created fear in his mind.

questions.question: Why did Mahendra avoid looking out of the window on full-moon nights?
questions.answer: Because he feared seeing the ghost described by Iswaran.

questions.question: What made Mahendra wake up in the night?
questions.answer: A strange moaning sound near his window.

questions.question: Why did Mahendra think the sound might be a cat?
questions.answer: Because cats often make sounds at night while searching for mice.

questions.question: What did Mahendra finally see outside the window?
questions.answer: A dark cloudy figure holding something.

questions.question: How did Mahendra try to explain the strange sight?
questions.answer: He thought it was auto-suggestion or imagination.

questions.question: What did Iswaran say the next morning about the ghost?
questions.answer: He said Mahendra had seen the female ghost.

questions.question: How did Mahendra react to Iswaran’s statement?
questions.answer: He felt a chill of fear.

questions.question: What decision did Mahendra make after the incident?
questions.answer: He decided to leave the place immediately.

questions.question: What role did imagination play in the story?
questions.answer: It created suspense and fear in Mahendra’s mind.

questions.question: Why is Iswaran called a fascinating storyteller?
questions.answer: Because he narrates stories with suspense and dramatic style.

questions.question: What quality helped Iswaran make ordinary events interesting?
questions.answer: His imaginative narration.

questions.question: What lesson does the story convey about imagination?
questions.answer: Imagination can strongly influence human thoughts and emotions.

questions.question: What literary element is strongly used in the story?
questions.answer: Suspense.

questions.question: What is the central idea of the story?
questions.answer: The power of storytelling and imagination.
